result.stdout:
Description:
  A CLI tool to manage your ADRs, RFCs, software design documents and more. See specific command help for more details about usage.

Usage:
  talo [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Increases log verbosity
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  config            Configures talo. Define new record types, set directory paths, prefixes and templates.
  init              Initialize talo in current directory. By default, it doesn't initialize any record types. See command help for more information.
  add, create, new  Add a new record of given type. Will create a new record from template and add it to the appropriate directory.
  link              Links two records to each other and updates statuses accordingly. See command help for usage.
  revise            Updates status of a record
  list              List record types and associated records
  export            Exports the records. By default exports all configured record types. See command help for details.

commands.payload:
Description:
  A CLI tool to manage your ADRs, RFCs, software design documents and more. See specific command help for more details about usage.

Usage:
  talo [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Increases log verbosity
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  config            Configures talo. Define new record types, set directory paths, prefixes and templates.
  init              Initialize talo in current directory. By default, it doesn't initialize any record types. See command help for more information.
  add, create, new  Add a new record of given type. Will create a new record from template and add it to the appropriate directory.
  link              Links two records to each other and updates statuses accordingly. See command help for usage.
  revise            Updates status of a record
  list              List record types and associated records
  export            Exports the records. By default exports all configured record types. See command help for details.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Configures talo. Define new record types, set directory paths, prefixes and templates.

Usage:
  talo config [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Increases log verbosity
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  adr  Architecture Decision Record
  rfc  Request for Comments
  add  Add new custom record type

commands.payload:
Description:
  Configures talo. Define new record types, set directory paths, prefixes and templates.

Usage:
  talo config [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Increases log verbosity
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  adr  Architecture Decision Record
  rfc  Request for Comments
  add  Add new custom record type

result.stdout:
Description:
  Add new custom record type

Usage:
  talo config add [options]

Options:
  -n, --name <name> (REQUIRED)                    Name of the new record type. Use all lower case and no white spaces.
  -d, --description <description>                 Description of the new record type
  -l, --location <location> (REQUIRED)            Directory location for the record type
  -t, --template-path <template-path> (REQUIRED)  Relative path to the template that will be used when creating new records
  -p, --prefix <prefix>                           Prefix for created files
  -v, --verbose                                   Increases log verbosity
  -?, -h, --help                                  Show help and usage information

Description:
  Add a new record of given type. Will create a new record from template and add it to the appropriate directory.

Usage:
  talo new [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Increases log verbosity
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  adr  Architecture Decision Record
  rfc  Request for Comments

commands.payload:
Description:
  Add a new record of given type. Will create a new record from template and add it to the appropriate directory.

Usage:
  talo new [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Increases log verbosity
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  adr  Architecture Decision Record
  rfc  Request for Comments

result.stdout:
Description:
  Architecture Decision Record

Usage:
  talo new adr [options]

Options:
  -t, --title <title> (REQUIRED)       Title for the new record
  -s, --status <status>                Desired status for the new record
  -u, --supersedes <supersedes>        Sequence number of the record that will be superseded by this record (positive integer)
  -f, --from-template <from-template>  Path of the custom template. If left empty, the template from configuration will be used
  -v, --verbose                        Increases log verbosity
  -?, -h, --help                       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Exports the records. By default exports all configured record types. See command help for details.

Usage:
  talo export [options]

Options:
  --types <adr|rfc>      List of record types to be exported. Multiple values supported.
  -o, --output <output>  Output directory for the export [default: export]
  -v, --verbose          Increases log verbosity
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Links two records to each other and updates statuses accordingly. See command help for usage.

Usage:
  talo link [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Increases log verbosity
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  adr  Architecture Decision Record
  rfc  Request for Comments

commands.payload:
Description:
  Links two records to each other and updates statuses accordingly. See command help for usage.

Usage:
  talo link [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Increases log verbosity
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  adr  Architecture Decision Record
  rfc  Request for Comments

result.stdout:
Description:
  Architecture Decision Record

Usage:
  talo link adr [options]

Options:
  -s, --source <source> (REQUIRED)                          Number of the source record (positive integer)
  -i, --source-status <source-status> (REQUIRED)            The status text that will be used to update the source record. For example, if source status value 'Amends' is given, the status of the source record will be updated to be 'Amends x' where x is the number of the destination record.
  -d, --destination <destination> (REQUIRED)                Number of the destination record (positive integer)
  -j, --destination-status <destination-status> (REQUIRED)  The status text that will be used to update the destination record. For example, if destination status value 'Amended by' is given, the status of the destination record will be updated to be 'Amended by y' where y is the number of the source record.
  -v, --verbose                                             Increases log verbosity
  -?, -h, --help                                            Show help and usage information

  -d, --destination <destination> (REQUIRED)                Number of the destination record (positive integer)

Options:
  -n, --number <number> (REQUIRED)  Sequence number of the revised record (positive integer)
  -s, --status <status> (REQUIRED)  Desired status for the new record
  -v, --verbose                     Increases log verbosity
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information
